Return
INDEPENDENT HOROLOGIST

Maciej
Miśnik

Fifth generation · Self-taught
WARSAW · KOŁOBRZEG · EST. MMXVII
MM—001 · THE ORIGIN

Born into
the Craft

December 24, 1991. Kołobrzeg—a Baltic port city. His father, a seaman, spent months on the open water. Four generations of watchmakers preceded him.

Nobody taught him. He learned from books, from silence, from the discipline of taking things apart.

1991
MM—002 · THE PHYSICIST

The Physicist's
Hands

PhD Physical Sciences, Gdańsk University of Technology. Research on thin layers of organic luminescent materials. Winner of the Diamond Grant.

He walked away from science to build the most complex micro-systems known to man.

MM—003 · THE INSTRUMENT

The Tourbillon

TYPE Pocket Watch
COMPONENTS 200+
BUILD TIME 1,000+ hrs
CNC Zero
MM—004 · THE LEGACY

A Watch for
the Seaman

His father spent months at sea. The marine chronometer Maciej built is a love letter to a childhood spent watching ships disappear over the horizon.

A tool of navigation, reimagined through five generations of craft.

"
Independence means freedom.
In the case of watchmaking, freedom
for me means being able to produce
every part at my own workshop.
MACIEJ MIŚNIK — QLEKTA, 2023
COORDINATES

Warsaw, Poland

INSTAGRAM @maciej_misnik
ACADEMY AHCI Profile
